core_model.structure_summary: A generative probabilistic model in which counts on higher-order interaction sets are Poisson-distributed with rates built from bilinear products of nonnegative node-membership vectors and a block-affinity matrix, and node attributes are Bernoulli with membership-mixed probabilities, all fit by maximum likelihood.
core_model.canonical_form: P(A_e\mid U,W) = \mathrm{Poisson}\!\left(\tfrac{\lambda_e}{\kappa_{|e|}}\right),\quad \lambda_e = \sum_{i,j\in e}\sum_{k,q} u_{ik}\,u_{jq}\,w_{kq}; \qquad P(x_{iz}\mid U,\beta)=\mathrm{Bernoulli}(\pi_{iz}),\ \pi_{iz}=\sum_k u_{ik}\beta_{kz}

variables.meaning: observed interaction counts on higher-order edges and binary attribute indicators on nodes
nonnegative per-node membership vectors, a block-to-block affinity matrix, and attribute-emission weights
the joint log-likelihood of interactions and attributes, maximized over the parameters

notes: Mixed-membership stochastic block model for attributed hypergraphs: a latent-variable generative model with Poisson higher-order edge likelihood (rate bilinear in membership vectors via u_i W u_j) and a Bernoulli attribute likelihood, estimated by maximizing the combined log-likelihood. 'probability_flow' flags that the model specifies distributions over the data; the dimensionality-reduction/visualization step is downstream and not the core structure. Attribute weights carry a simplex normalization (sum_k beta_kz = 1).
